title: scry

features.title: Persisted queries (hash → query indirection)
features.description:
Operator pre-registers expensive queries via CLI; scry stores
the SHA-256(query) → query text map per upstream. Agents call
`query_execute(server, hash="...")` instead of pushing the full
query string every time. Cuts agent context budget + upstream
payload + audit chain footprint for known-shape workloads
(dashboards, batch ETL, recurring planner calls).

CLI:
  scry pq add <server> <name> --file query.graphql
  scry pq list <server>
  scry pq remove <server> <hash|name>

Storage: per-server SQLite table in the existing index DB so
hashes + bodies survive restarts + replicate with the schema
index. Hot-reload on `pq add` (no MCP reconnect needed).

Mapping precedence in query_execute: `hash` arg wins → look up,
404 if missing. `query` arg → existing path. Both → conflict
error.

features.title: Apollo federation awareness in schema search
features.description:
Detect federated upstreams (probe `_service { sdl }` during
introspection). When present, parse the subgraph SDL and tag
each type/field with its subgraph name. schema_search renders
the subgraph tag alongside results so agents see which subgraph
owns a `Customer.email` vs `Customer.preferredCarrier`.

Cross-subgraph queries still hit the federated gateway URL the
operator configured; scry doesn't reroute or compose. Just
surfaces ownership.

features.title: Per-server cache hit/miss metrics
features.description:
v0.3 shipped the result cache without metric instrumentation.
Add `scry.cache.hits_total{server}` + `scry.cache.misses_total{server}`
counters wired through query_execute. Bypass calls (mutations
effect=write) don't increment either. Operators wire to their
monitor; agents now have a feedback signal on whether their
dedupe is working.

features.title: gqlparser / gate / cache fuzz harness
features.description:
Build-tagged fuzz tests over the GraphQL parsing surface:
`schema.ParseSDL`, `gate.Classify`, `cache.Key`. Run with
`go test -fuzz` locally + a nightly CI workflow that fuzzes
for 5 minutes per target. Catches crashes from adversarial
inputs (malformed SDL, weird unicode in queries, gigantic
operationName) before they reach prod.

Discovered crashers go into testdata/fuzz/ for replay; nightly
run is non-blocking on PRs.

features.title: mTLS subject → MCP Identity propagation
features.description:
When --mtls-ca is set + the transport receives a verified
client cert, extract the subject CN (plus first DNS SAN as
fallback) and surface it as `Identity.Name` in the MCP
request context. Lets clients.yml scope by cert subject
instead of (or alongside) a shared-secret token —
service-mesh deployments where identity already lives in
SPIFFE/X509 certs get a native authorisation path.

Implementation: HTTP middleware that wraps the transport
handler chain and writes the cert-derived Identity onto
ctx before the BearerAuth middleware runs. Bearer + mTLS
compose: if both present, mTLS subject wins (the cert is
the strong identity).
